methodsAttempted.query: site:une.edu.au (generative AI OR artificial intelligence OR ChatGPT OR GenAI) policy academic integrity assessment students University of New England Australia
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found public UNE myLearn records for Generative AI and Academic Integrity, UNE news/explainers, and the official HDR artificial intelligence guidance page.
Firecrawl search found the public academic integrity myLearn section, the login-gated GenAI course shell, the HDR AI guidance page, and School of Education myLearn guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:policies.une.edu.au artificial intelligence generative AI ChatGPT academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: No direct AI-specific result was returned from the UNE policy register domain search.

methodsAttempted.query: site:www.une.edu.au "Student Academic Integrity Policy" "University of New England"
methodsAttempted.notes: Search located official UNE library and enrolment pages that link to the Student Academic Integrity Policy.

methodsAttempted.query: UNE Student Academic Integrity Policy current id 00304 artificial intelligence tool
methodsAttempted.notes: The current Student Academic Integrity Policy was fetched successfully through Firecrawl and includes an artificial-intelligence-tool clause in the contract cheating example.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a current formal Student Academic Integrity Policy, a substantive public UNE HDR generative AI guidance page, and public myLearn pages that describe UNE's academic integrity and generative AI module resources. The full Generative AI and Academic Integrity course content required UNE login/self-enrolment and was not used as evidence beyond the public login-shell status. A general terms-of-enrolment page was rejected for low AI specificity.

targets.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity | Learning Online at UNE
School of Education Information Site: UNE Academic Integrity Policy

stopConditions: Use only official UNE-controlled pages as evidence.
Do not bypass the login/self-enrolment wall on the full Generative AI and Academic Integrity course.
Scope School of Education evidence to that myLearn information site and do not present it as a university-wide policy.
Scope HDR guidance to higher degree research candidates.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: This site requires you to login with your UNE account. Please press Continue to try logging in with your UNE details.
artifacts.notes: Full course contents were not accessed because the site required UNE account login/self-enrolment.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: You must be aware of the principles of academic integrity and for managing student breaches of academic integrity at UNE by reading and complying with the Student Academic Integrity Policy.
artifacts.notes: Official page links academic integrity obligations but does not itself state AI-specific policy or guidance.

artifacts.errorReason: Public course shell required UNE account login/self-enrolment for full content.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity | Learning Online at UNE
School of Education Information Site: UNE Academic Integrity Policy

artifacts.claimText: UNE's Student Academic Integrity Policy includes artificial intelligence tools in its example of work substantially written by someone else, within the policy's breach framework for undergraduate and postgraduate award and non-award coursework students; the policy states it does not apply to higher degree by research courses.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI tools included in coursework academic integrity breach examples; HDR excluded from this policy

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This Policy applies to all students enrolled in undergraduate and postgraduate award and non-award courses offered by UNE... The Policy does not apply to higher degree by research courses. Breaches... include... work substantially written by someone else... artificial intelligence tool, or other tool.

artifacts.claimText: UNE's HDR generative AI guidance says higher degree research candidates are required to consider ethical, critical, and creative uses of generative AI and to ensure proper citation of content generated by AI tools and sources they use.
artifacts.normalizedValue: HDR candidates must acknowledge and cite generative AI use

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In the planning of a research project, HDR candidates are required to consider the ethical, critical, and creative uses of generative AI and to ensure proper citation of all content generated by AI tools and sources that you use.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Should HDR candidates use Generative AI in Research?

artifacts.claimText: UNE's HDR generative AI guidance warns that failure to reference generative AI content may be considered academic or research misconduct, and lists risks including unacknowledged AI output, inaccurate AI-generated references, substantive AI-generated thesis or publication content, peer review or ethics-clearance use, and disclosure of sensitive or confidential material to generative AI.
artifacts.normalizedValue: HDR GenAI non-acknowledgement and specified uses may raise misconduct risks

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Failure to reference content from generative AI may be considered academic misconduct or research misconduct... avoid... Using output from generative AI tools without acknowledgment... Including substantive content from a generative AI tool into your HDR thesis or publication... Breaching data privacy, security, copyright, intellectual property, or confidentiality...
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Should HDR candidates use Generative AI in Research?

artifacts.claimText: UNE's HDR generative AI guidance cautions that inputting research data into AI can risk loss of copyright or intellectual property control and can have serious implications if sensitive or highly sensitive data is inadvertently released.
artifacts.normalizedValue: HDR research data input to AI is flagged as privacy/IP risk

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Inputting any research data into AI, is at the risk of losing copyright/intellectual property rights... This could have significant ramifications... if you inadvertently release sensitive or highly sensitive data.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: The nature of your research project

artifacts.claimText: UNE's public Learning Online academic integrity myLearn section states that a Generative AI and Academic Integrity at UNE module exists and lists module topics including general principles for use of generative AI, Turnitin detection and AI, and referencing generative AI.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Public myLearn source confirms GenAI academic integrity module topics, but full module content is separate

citation.citationTitle: Academic Integrity | Learning Online at UNE

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity | Learning Online at UNE
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Discover Generative AI and Academic Integrity at UNE and learn UNE's stance and how you can ethically use Generative AI... Generative AI Modules: What is Generative AI? General Principles for Use of Generative AI. TurnItIn Detection and AI. Referencing Generative AI. Example Case Study.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Academic Integrity section, Generative AI Modules

citation.citationTitle: Academic Integrity | Learning Online at UNE

artifacts.claimText: A UNE School of Education public myLearn information page tells students that computer programs, artificial intelligence tools, or other tools should not be used to write or produce any part of an assessment response unless explicitly referenced and in line with unit coordinator advice for the assessment task.
artifacts.normalizedValue: School of Education page: AI assessment use requires explicit referencing and unit coordinator advice

citation.citationTitle: School of Education Information Site: UNE Academic Integrity Policy

artifacts.sourceTitle: School of Education Information Site: UNE Academic Integrity Policy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: tools including computer programs, artificial intelligence tools or other tools should NOT be used to write, or produce, any part of your assessment response unless it has been explicitly referenced and in line with advice by the unit coordinator in your assessment task.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: School of Education Information Site page text

citation.citationTitle: School of Education Information Site: UNE Academic Integrity Policy

artifacts.notes: Approve as a narrow formal-policy claim scoped to coursework students and the policy's stated HDR exclusion.
Approve as HDR-scoped guidance directly supported by the official Graduate Research School page.
Approve as HDR-scoped integrity risk guidance; wording avoids making the page broader than the HDR context.
Approve as HDR-scoped data/privacy risk guidance tied to the source's research-data paragraph.
Approve as source-status evidence about public module availability and topics, not as a full module-content claim.
Approve only with School of Education scope; do not promote as a university-wide assessment policy without further source support.

artifacts.title: University of New England Australia AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official UNE sources support staged candidate claims for coursework academic-integrity treatment of artificial intelligence tools, HDR generative AI acknowledgement and risk guidance, public myLearn source-status evidence for a Generative AI and Academic Integrity module, and School of Education-scoped assessment guidance. The full GenAI module content required UNE login/self-enrolment and was not used as claim evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The full Generative AI and Academic Integrity at UNE course content was behind a UNE account login/self-enrolment screen and was not accessed.
School of Education myLearn guidance is scoped to that information site and should not be presented as a university-wide policy without additional official support.
